title: mithridatea toxicaria
intro: Mithridatea toxicaria, also known as the poison nut or poison tree, is a plant species native to the tropical regions of Central and South America, characterized by its toxic seeds and leaves. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and analgesic effects, as well as its ability to treat fever and snake bites. The health benefits of Mithridatea toxicaria are attributed to its active compounds, including annonacin and annona toxins, which have been shown to exhibit antioxidant and antiparasitic properties. In herbal preparations, the plant's seeds and leaves are often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, although caution is advised due to its toxic nature.
